characters.physical_details: Broad-shouldered; chin towing-hook scar; rope-rough hands.

key_relationships.iven_an: brother she distrusts but keeps using
key_relationships.nami_an: youngest sister she once left behind and now refuses to leave again

characters.occupation_status: Former Halcote salvage pilot; stays at Knot Bend to run the rebuilt crossing while Pike's debt remains.
characters.literacy_languages: Reads route notes and work tallies; speaks Reedmouth dialect and inland standard.

state_transitions.change: Receives Tovin's iron key and becomes the route's practical leader.
Loses the Bitter Thread in the Coil Reach fire and shifts to the old funeral ferry.
Signs away her Halcote berth and room to restructure debt and commits to the communal Knot Bend crossing.

characters.availability: Alive; based at Knot Bend after chapter_18.

characters.physical_details: Reed-thin; striking mouth and throat; singer's hands marked by cuts.

key_relationships.riva_an: older sister and rival over authority
key_relationships.nami_an: sister whose need for chosenness mirrors his own
key_relationships.sereya_an: mother whose charisma and damage he repeats

characters.occupation_status: Former funeral singer; after Storm Mouth he keeps ferry time by frame drum instead of voice.
characters.literacy_languages: Reads hymns and simple ledgers; speaks Reedmouth dialect, inland standard, and river cant.

state_transitions.change: Ilyan publicly claims him as his son and Hymn Cut answers his singing.
Publicly offers himself as the single heir and splits boats away from Riva's labor plan.
Joins the collective rite, destroys his singing voice, and loses any credible one-heir claim.

characters.availability: Alive; present throughout; voice permanently damaged after chapter_17.

characters.physical_details: Narrow-faced; indigo-stained nails; old needle scar in the thumb.

key_relationships.nami_an: sister she left behind and now protects

characters.occupation_status: Bookkeeper, dyer, and route mapper for the rebuilt Knot Bend crossing.
characters.literacy_languages: Fully literate in trade script and route records; speaks Reedmouth dialect and inland standard.

state_transitions.change: Receives Paska's tide-map tattoo and admits tearing the missing page after Sereya's burial.
Uses grain hall lights, drains, and lanes to hold Halcote's sleepwalkers.
Keeps the public maintenance ledger and remaps the reopened channels.

characters.availability: Alive; present throughout; usually at the ledger table after chapter_18.

characters.physical_details: Gold tooth, quick hands, faded river tattoos, loose insolent posture.

key_relationships.lale_an: sister who keeps his count honest
key_relationships.sereya_an: mother whose appetite he learned to perform

characters.occupation_status: Cardsharp and smuggler turned one-handed rope trainee under Enver; Kes Varn debt still stands.
characters.literacy_languages: Reads route marks and gambling tallies; speaks Reedmouth dialect and market slang.

state_transitions.change: Incurs a 62-mark debt and two favors to Kes Varn after the Marrow Market corpse jam.
Admits he sold the missing ledger page and returns it to the family.
Left hand is mangled at Storm Mouth's capstan while swinging the funeral ferry into place.
Learns one-handed rope-guide work under Enver.

characters.availability: Alive; present throughout; left hand permanently damaged after chapter_16.

characters.physical_details: Small-framed; ankle rash scars; wide dark eyes; usually smells of brine and smoke.

key_relationships.riva_an: older sister who left and returned
key_relationships.daro_an: brother whose hearing tempts her toward chosenness
key_relationships.bato_serin: likely father who kills her martyr fantasy
key_relationships.sereya_an: mother remembered mostly through other people's stories

characters.occupation_status: Crossing helper and seed-bed keeper beside the rebuilt house; still convalescing from the chapter_08 wound.
characters.literacy_languages: Reads simple notices slowly; speaks Reedmouth dialect and enough inland standard for wage work.

state_transitions.change: Secretly pockets a salt-fig seed after the second drowned body reaches Knot Bend.
Cuts her hand on reed glass; the wound infects and becomes a dangerous fever.
Reveals the hidden seed and learns from Bato that chosenness is not a crown.
Ties the hidden seed into the collective rite and rejects sacrificial self-selection.
Starts clean nursery beds beside the rebuilt crossing.

characters.availability: Alive; present throughout; weakest from chapters 8-12 and working again by chapter_18.

characters.physical_details: Black-haired, broad-hipped, strong at the pole, impossible to ignore in a room.

key_relationships.riva_an: mother who trained usefulness as neglect
key_relationships.daro_an: mother who fused charisma to need

characters.occupation_status: Deceased ferryman-saint whose charisma kept the route fed and badly mythologized.
characters.literacy_languages: Read route marks and ledgers; spoke Reedmouth dialect, inland standard, and ritual cant.

state_transitions.change: Jun's restored page turns her saint legend into a record of failed sacrifice.
Bato's testimony and later action strip away the one-heir doctrine she tried to build.

characters.availability: Dead before chapter_01; present only through rumor, record, relics, and inherited damage.

characters.physical_details: Burn-scarred forearms, flattened nose, arthritic hands, eel-smoke clothes.

key_relationships.jun_an: witness to the failed rite's aftermath

characters.occupation_status: Keeper of Reed Gate and co-builder of the rebuilt Knot Bend crossing.
characters.literacy_languages: Reads route marks and simple ledgers; speaks Reedmouth dialect and trader speech.

state_transitions.change: Summons the siblings back to Reedmouth.
Hands Riva the iron key after Reed Gate reopens.
Runs the Storm Mouth wheel housing and insists on the shared sequence.

characters.availability: Alive; moving between Reed Gate and Knot Bend.

characters.physical_details: Tall, blind, voice-rich; milky scar tissue over both eyes.
characters.literacy_languages: Dictates records; speaks Reedmouth dialect, inland standard, and ceremonial cant.

state_transitions.change: Publicly claims Daro and opens Hymn Cut with him.

characters.availability: Alive at Hymn Cut; offpage after chapter_05.

characters.physical_details: Lean; scarred forearms; ink-dark nails; one temple shaved.
characters.occupation_status: Tattooist and tidal mapkeeper at Marrow Market.
characters.literacy_languages: Reads and writes trade script and route marks; speaks Reedmouth dialect and inland standard.

state_transitions.change: Tattoos Lale, restores the 'sixth mouth' memory, and explains why Sereya distributed the route.

characters.availability: Alive at Marrow Market; available for route corrections.

characters.physical_details: Big-chested; smoke-dark beard; rope-burned palms; damp-weather limp.
characters.occupation_status: Ropewalk owner at Coil Reach; trains Iven after the flood.
characters.literacy_languages: Reads invoices and shipping marks; speaks Reedmouth dialect and trader standard.

state_transitions.change: Publicly names Iven as his son and loses the ropehouse to fire.
Helps rebuild Knot Bend and teaches Iven one-handed line work.

characters.availability: Alive; mostly at Coil Reach and Knot Bend after chapter_11.

characters.physical_details: Salt-scarred face, one shoulder lower than the other, nearly white hair.
characters.occupation_status: Storm diver and hidden Salt Room keeper until he returns to guide the final opening.
characters.literacy_languages: Reads tide marks and simple charts; speaks Reedmouth dialect and rough sea-trader speech.

state_transitions.change: Reveals himself alive and rejects singular sainthood.
Guides the five-point opening of Storm Mouth.

characters.availability: Alive; hidden until chapter_14, then present for sea-edge route work.

characters.physical_details: Infant with black hair, Bato's feet, and a storm rash on the chest.

key_relationships.lale_an: name she removed from the ledger

characters.occupation_status: Deceased infant; continuity relevance is familial and ritual.

state_transitions.change: The restored page returns his name to the family record.
His loss stops functioning as a model for another child sacrifice.

characters.availability: Dead before chapter_01; present only through memory, record, and river pressure.

timeline.story_start: Late monsoon, seven years after Sereya's burial, when returned drowned begin reaching Knot Bend with salt-fig seeds in their mouths.
timeline.estimated_span: About five weeks: roughly two storm-heavy weeks to reopen the route, then a three-week low-water rebuild.

seasonal_track.season: Monsoon rise and first backwash failures.
High monsoon, when rooted seeds start driving the living toward water.
Flood crest and sea-breach work at Storm Mouth.

key_events.event: Returned drowned child appears at the House of Oaths with a salt-fig seed.
Reed Gate reopens and Tovin gives Riva the iron key.
Ilyan publicly claims Daro and Hymn Cut opens.
Marrow Market's corpse jam creates Iven's Kes Varn debt.
Jun's page is recovered and read, exposing the failed Storm Mouth attempt.
The Bitter Thread burns and the family shifts onto Sereya's old funeral ferry.
Lale turns Halcote's grain hall into a holding mouth for sleepwalkers.
Bato reveals himself alive and states that no single heir can hold Storm Mouth.
Storm Mouth is revealed as a five-point lock; Iven's left hand is mangled at the landing capstan.
The five siblings open the sea road together; Daro loses his singing voice.
Knot Bend becomes a working freight and burial crossing with public ledgers and shared maintenance.

geography.primary_setting: Reedmouth Delta and the linked inland Halcote basin between the Ravel and the Gray Sea.

key_locations.name: House of Oaths at Knot Bend
key_locations.details: Sereya's former shrine-home; by chapter_18 it is rebuilt as a shared ferry crossing with a public maintenance ledger and hanging key.

key_locations.name: Reed Gate and Tovin's Eel Yards
key_locations.details: Iron-toothed first sluice and work yard where the route is mechanically restarted.

key_locations.name: Hymn Cut
key_locations.details: Ilyan's mudbank shrine with bell posts, pilgrim cots, and a channel that answers to song once truth is spoken.

key_locations.name: Marrow Market and Paska's Ink House
key_locations.details: Public bottleneck of fish trade, tattoos, and route memory; later site of the corpse jam and rooted seeds.

key_locations.name: Lantern Quay
key_locations.details: Fishing village with communal suppers, prayer bowls, and the recovered ledger page.

key_locations.name: Coil Reach Ropewalk
key_locations.details: Enver's ropehouse and tension-cut yard; the Bitter Thread burns here.

key_locations.name: Halcote Basin, Quarry, and Grain Hall
key_locations.details: Inland brick-and-grain town whose flooded drains pull sleepwalkers uphill toward the quarry until Lale redirects them into the hall.

key_locations.name: Salt Room and Storm Mouth
key_locations.details: Sea-edge ruined chapel and black breach where river and tide meet through a five-point lock.

distances: Knot Bend is about a half-day pole from Reed Gate in ordinary water.
Reed Gate and Hymn Cut are close enough to run in the same early flood sequence.
Marrow Market sits on the public bottleneck between Inkwater and Lantern Quay.
Halcote lies inland above the delta, with walkers drawn toward its flooded quarry drains.
Salt Room is at the sea edge; once the reed islands break, larger hulls lose maneuvering room there.

world_rules: Returned drowned carrying salt-fig seeds mean the sea road is blocked and pressure is backing inland.
Rooted salt-fig seeds push nearby living people into sleepwalking toward water.
Cuts answer to sequence, labor, and recognized bonds or tokens; spectacle alone destabilizes them.
Storm Mouth is a five-point lock, not a one-heir gate; all five sibling lines must answer for a clean opening.
There is no permanent saintly shortcut: the sea road only holds through ongoing maintenance.
River signs are real but amplify grief, desire, and panic as easily as duty.
Night flood travel is materially worse because submerged posts, broken reed islands, and backwash hide the true path.

power_structure.holder: The Ravel, monsoon crest, and Gray Sea tide
power_structure.over: Reedmouth, Lantern Quay, Halcote, and every route traveler
power_structure.mechanism: Flood timing, returned drowned, backwash, and sleepwalker pressure.

power_structure.over: Reed Gate sequencing and final-wheel procedure
power_structure.mechanism: Mechanical knowledge, control of the first key, and local trust in her maintenance authority.

power_structure.over: Hymn Cut mourners and ritual legitimacy there
power_structure.mechanism: Shrine custom, voice, and public recognition of Daro.

power_structure.holder: Enver Coil and related debt networks
power_structure.over: Rope, capstans, repair gear, and some dock labor
power_structure.mechanism: Material control, rope expertise, and brokered obligations.

power_structure.holder: The Knot Bend crossing ledger and publicly hung key
power_structure.mechanism: Shared schedules, freight books, public access to the crossing, and labor that no longer depends on one sainted body.

objects.name: Sereya's reed ledger
objects.owner: The An siblings and the Knot Bend crossing table
objects.origin: Route book listing lovers, cut order, and later maintenance notes.
objects.status: Found in chapter_02, restored with Jun's page in chapters 09-10, and used as the crossing ledger source in chapter_18.

objects.name: The missing ledger page naming Jun
objects.owner: The An siblings after being held by Sena Dey
objects.origin: Torn page describing Jun and Sereya's failed Storm Mouth attempt.
objects.status: Removed by Lale after Sereya's burial, sold by Iven, recovered in chapter_09, read in chapter_10, and sewn back into the ledger.

objects.name: Tovin's iron gate key
objects.owner: Publicly hung at the rebuilt House of Oaths after passing from Tovin to Riva
objects.origin: Original Reed Gate tool forged for the old lock system.
objects.status: Used at Reed Gate and again at Storm Mouth; hung beside the maintenance ledger in chapter_18.

objects.name: The Bitter Thread
objects.origin: Mortgaged Halcote salvage dredge brought back to Reedmouth.
objects.status: Destroyed in the Coil Reach fire and sink in chapter_11.

objects.name: Sereya's old funeral ferry
objects.origin: Death-ferry hull kept at the House of Oaths.
objects.status: Becomes the main vessel after chapter_11 and remains the working crossing ferry in chapter_18.

objects.name: Sereya's brass-ferruled pole
objects.owner: Communal route gear after Daro's theft fails to make it singular
objects.origin: Sereya's personal ferry pole preserved as a relic.
objects.status: Stolen in chapter_14, used in Daro's schism and in the final rite, then reduced from relic to shared tool.

objects.name: Salt-fig seeds
objects.owner: No stable owner; one is carried by Nami until chapter_17
objects.origin: Seeds returned in the mouths of the drowned when the sea road is blocked.
objects.status: Rooting threat through the flood crest; Nami's hidden seed is tied into the chapter_17 rite and the remaining seeds go inert once the sea road opens.

debts.amount: 480 silver marks plus repair interest on the lost Bitter Thread
debts.deadline: Originally the post-monsoon fair; restructured in chapter_18 against Riva's Halcote berth, room, and stored yard tackle, with remaining interest frozen until winter fair
debts.status: Partially settled by surrendering inland assets; liability remains.
Incurred in chapter_06 to free Marrow Market gear; still outstanding by chapter_18.

income_sources.source: Knot Bend freight and burial crossings
income_sources.amount: Small but growing cash flow after chapter_18
income_sources.reliability: Improving if the route stays maintained

income_sources.amount: Steady modest cash and trade credit

income_sources.reliability: Ended after the chapter_17 throat injury

income_sources.source: Enver's rope work and Iven's training
income_sources.amount: In-kind support now, possible wages later

knowledge_state.knows: The route must reopen in lover order from Reed Gate to Storm Mouth to stop the returned drowned.
knowledge_state.hidden_from: Most delta and inland towns at first

knowledge_state.knows: He has heard the drowned since childhood and mistook that attention for calling.
She tore the ledger page after Sereya's burial because it named a 'sixth mouth.'
He sold the torn page to Sena Dey for six silver marks and a bottle, then later retrieved it.
Jun drowned during Sereya and Bato's failed Storm Mouth attempt and Sereya scattered the remaining children afterward to avoid choosing another heir.
Jun was Sereya and Bato's infant son, lost in the failed Storm Mouth holding.
knowledge_state.hidden_from: Reedmouth at first; later leaks into rumor

knowledge_state.knows: Storm Mouth is a recurring five-point lock and no single heir can permanently hold it.
She kept a salt-fig seed for proof of chosenness until tying it into the final rite.

environmental_constants: Reedmouth and Knot Bend smell of wet wood, eel smoke, rot, tar, and iron-rich mud.
Channels usually change sound before they change shape: breathing, suction, and under-song warn first.
Floodwater leaves salt, reed pollen, fish slime, and plant matter clinging to skin, rope, and timber.
At the sea edge, salt crust forms on stone and rope, and the air tastes cleaner but more dangerous.
Low water reveals damage instead of restoring normalcy: exposed posts, dead animals, brackish garden beds, and warped sills.
